import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

df = pd.read_csv('dataset.csv')

print("=== SHAPE ===")
print(df.shape)

print("\n=== DTYPES ===")
print(df.dtypes)

print("\n=== NULL COUNTS ===")
print(df.isnull().sum())

print("\n=== BASIC STATS ===")
print(df.describe())

print("\n=== CATEGORICAL VALUE COUNTS ===")
for col in ['batch', 'technician']:
    print(f"\n{col}:")
    print(df[col].value_counts())

print("\n=== PER-BATCH STATS FOR DOSAGE & RESPONSE ===")
for batch in sorted(df['batch'].unique()):
    sub = df[df['batch'] == batch]
    print(f"\n{batch}:")
    print(f"  dosage: mean={sub['dosage_mg'].mean():.2f}, std={sub['dosage_mg'].std():.2f}, "
          f"min={sub['dosage_mg'].min()}, max={sub['dosage_mg'].max()}")
    print(f"  response: mean={sub['response'].mean():.2f}, std={sub['response'].std():.2f}")
    # Linear regression stats
    from scipy import stats
    slope, intercept, r_value, p_value, std_err = stats.linregress(sub['dosage_mg'], sub['response'])
    print(f"  linear fit: y = {slope:.3f}x + {intercept:.3f}, R²={r_value**2:.3f}, p={p_value:.4f}")

print("\n=== CORRELATION MATRIX (numeric) ===")
print(df[['dosage_mg', 'response', 'lab_temp_c', 'weight_kg']].corr())

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from scipy import stats

df = pd.read_csv('dataset.csv')

# ================================================================
# PLOT 1: Per-batch scatterplots with regression lines (the key plot)
# ================================================================
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(12, 10))
batches = sorted(df['batch'].unique())
titles = {
    'batch_Q1': 'Q1: Linear relationship',
    'batch_Q2': 'Q2: Non-linear (quadratic)',
    'batch_Q3': 'Q3: Outlier in y',
    'batch_Q4': 'Q4: Outlier in x (leverage point)'
}

for ax, batch in zip(axes.flat, batches):
    sub = df[df['batch'] == batch]
    ax.scatter(sub['dosage_mg'], sub['response'], alpha=0.8, s=60, edgecolor='k', linewidth=0.5)
    
    # Fit and plot regression line
    slope, intercept, r_value, p_value, std_err = stats.linregress(sub['dosage_mg'], sub['response'])
    x_line = np.linspace(sub['dosage_mg'].min() - 1, sub['dosage_mg'].max() + 1, 100)
    ax.plot(x_line, slope * x_line + intercept, 'r--', alpha=0.7, linewidth=2,
            label=f'y={slope:.2f}x+{intercept:.2f}\nR²={r_value**2:.3f}')
    
    ax.set_title(titles[batch], fontsize=13, fontweight='bold')
    ax.set_xlabel('Dosage (mg)')
    ax.set_ylabel('Response')
    ax.legend(fontsize=9)
    ax.set_xlim(2, 20)
    ax.set_ylim(2, 14)

fig.suptitle("Anscombe's Quartet: Identical Statistics, Different Data\n"
             "(All have: mean_x=9, mean_y=7.5, slope=0.50, R²=0.667)",
             fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout(rect=[0, 0, 1, 0.93])
plt.savefig('plots/01_anscombe_scatterplots.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 1 saved.")

# ================================================================
# PLOT 2: Residual diagnostics per batch
# ================================================================
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 4, figsize=(16, 8))

for i, batch in enumerate(batches):
    sub = df[df['batch'] == batch].copy()
    slope, intercept, r_value, _, _ = stats.linregress(sub['dosage_mg'], sub['response'])
    sub['predicted'] = slope * sub['dosage_mg'] + intercept
    sub['residual'] = sub['response'] - sub['predicted']
    
    # Residual vs fitted
    ax = axes[0, i]
    ax.scatter(sub['predicted'], sub['residual'], alpha=0.8, s=50, edgecolor='k', linewidth=0.5)
    ax.axhline(0, color='red', linestyle='--', alpha=0.5)
    ax.set_title(f'{batch}\nResiduals vs Fitted', fontsize=10)
    ax.set_xlabel('Fitted values')
    ax.set_ylabel('Residual')
    
    # QQ plot
    ax2 = axes[1, i]
    stats.probplot(sub['residual'], plot=ax2)
    ax2.set_title(f'{batch}\nQ-Q Plot', fontsize=10)

fig.suptitle('Regression Diagnostics: Residual Analysis Per Batch', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout(rect=[0, 0, 1, 0.94])
plt.savefig('plots/02_residual_diagnostics.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 2 saved.")

# ================================================================
# PLOT 3: Distribution overview
# ================================================================
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(12, 9))

# Dosage distribution by batch
ax = axes[0, 0]
for batch in batches:
    sub = df[df['batch'] == batch]
    ax.hist(sub['dosage_mg'], bins=8, alpha=0.5, label=batch, edgecolor='k')
ax.set_title('Dosage Distribution by Batch')
ax.set_xlabel('Dosage (mg)')
ax.legend()

# Response distribution by batch
ax = axes[0, 1]
for batch in batches:
    sub = df[df['batch'] == batch]
    ax.hist(sub['response'], bins=8, alpha=0.5, label=batch, edgecolor='k')
ax.set_title('Response Distribution by Batch')
ax.set_xlabel('Response')
ax.legend()

# Lab temp boxplot
ax = axes[1, 0]
df.boxplot(column='lab_temp_c', by='batch', ax=ax)
ax.set_title('Lab Temperature by Batch')
ax.set_xlabel('Batch')
ax.set_ylabel('Temp (°C)')
plt.sca(ax)
plt.title('Lab Temperature by Batch')

# Weight boxplot
ax = axes[1, 1]
df.boxplot(column='weight_kg', by='batch', ax=ax)
ax.set_title('Subject Weight by Batch')
ax.set_xlabel('Batch')
ax.set_ylabel('Weight (kg)')
plt.sca(ax)
plt.title('Subject Weight by Batch')

fig.suptitle('Variable Distributions', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout(rect=[0, 0, 1, 0.94])
plt.savefig('plots/03_distributions.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 3 saved.")

# ================================================================
# PLOT 4: Covariate analysis (lab_temp, weight, technician)
# ================================================================
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(15, 5))

# Response vs lab_temp
ax = axes[0]
for batch in batches:
    sub = df[df['batch'] == batch]
    ax.scatter(sub['lab_temp_c'], sub['response'], alpha=0.7, label=batch, s=50)
ax.set_xlabel('Lab Temperature (°C)')
ax.set_ylabel('Response')
ax.set_title('Response vs Lab Temperature')
ax.legend()

# Response vs weight
ax = axes[1]
for batch in batches:
    sub = df[df['batch'] == batch]
    ax.scatter(sub['weight_kg'], sub['response'], alpha=0.7, label=batch, s=50)
ax.set_xlabel('Weight (kg)')
ax.set_ylabel('Response')
ax.set_title('Response vs Weight')
ax.legend()

# Response by technician
ax = axes[2]
sns.boxplot(data=df, x='technician', y='response', ax=ax)
ax.set_title('Response by Technician')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/04_covariate_analysis.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 4 saved.")

print("\nAll plots generated.")

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from scipy import stats
import statsmodels.api as sm

df = pd.read_csv('dataset.csv')
batches = sorted(df['batch'].unique())

print("=" * 70)
print("DETAILED PER-BATCH REGRESSION DIAGNOSTICS")
print("=" * 70)

for batch in batches:
    sub = df[df['batch'] == batch].copy()
    X = sm.add_constant(sub['dosage_mg'])
    model = sm.OLS(sub['response'], X).fit()
    
    print(f"\n{'='*50}")
    print(f"  {batch}")
    print(f"{'='*50}")
    print(model.summary())
    
    # Normality test on residuals
    resid = model.resid
    shapiro_stat, shapiro_p = stats.shapiro(resid)
    print(f"\nShapiro-Wilk normality test: stat={shapiro_stat:.4f}, p={shapiro_p:.4f}")
    
    # Breusch-Pagan heteroscedasticity test
    from statsmodels.stats.diagnostic import het_breuschpagan
    bp_stat, bp_p, _, _ = het_breuschpagan(resid, X)
    print(f"Breusch-Pagan test: stat={bp_stat:.4f}, p={bp_p:.4f}")
    
    # Durbin-Watson
    from statsmodels.stats.stattools import durbin_watson
    dw = durbin_watson(resid)
    print(f"Durbin-Watson: {dw:.4f}")
    
    # Influence measures
    influence = model.get_influence()
    cooks_d = influence.cooks_distance[0]
    leverage = influence.hat_matrix_diag
    print(f"Max Cook's distance: {cooks_d.max():.4f} (observation idx: {cooks_d.argmax()})")
    print(f"Max leverage: {leverage.max():.4f}")
    
    # Check for non-linearity: quadratic term
    X_quad = sm.add_constant(np.column_stack([sub['dosage_mg'], sub['dosage_mg']**2]))
    model_quad = sm.OLS(sub['response'], X_quad).fit()
    # F-test for quadratic term significance
    print(f"\nQuadratic term p-value: {model_quad.pvalues[2]:.4f}")
    print(f"Quadratic model R²: {model_quad.rsquared:.4f}")
    print(f"Linear model R²: {model.rsquared:.4f}")
    print(f"AIC linear: {model.aic:.2f}, AIC quadratic: {model_quad.aic:.2f}")

print("\n" + "=" * 70)
print("ANOVA: RESPONSE BY BATCH (controlling for dosage)")
print("=" * 70)
# Does batch matter after controlling for dosage?
df['dosage_sq'] = df['dosage_mg'] ** 2
X_full = pd.get_dummies(df[['dosage_mg', 'batch']], columns=['batch'], drop_first=True, dtype=float)
X_full = sm.add_constant(X_full)
model_full = sm.OLS(df['response'], X_full).fit()
print(model_full.summary())

print("\n" + "=" * 70)
print("COVARIATE EFFECTS: lab_temp_c, weight_kg, technician")
print("=" * 70)
# Check if covariates matter
for cov in ['lab_temp_c', 'weight_kg']:
    r, p = stats.pearsonr(df[cov], df['response'])
    print(f"  {cov} vs response: r={r:.3f}, p={p:.3f}")

# Technician effect via ANOVA
groups = [df[df['technician'] == t]['response'] for t in df['technician'].unique()]
f_stat, f_p = stats.f_oneway(*groups)
print(f"  Technician ANOVA: F={f_stat:.3f}, p={f_p:.3f}")

# Kruskal-Wallis (non-parametric)
h_stat, h_p = stats.kruskal(*groups)
print(f"  Technician Kruskal-Wallis: H={h_stat:.3f}, p={h_p:.3f}")

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from scipy import stats
import statsmodels.api as sm
from statsmodels.stats.diagnostic import het_breuschpagan
from statsmodels.stats.stattools import durbin_watson

df = pd.read_csv('dataset.csv')
batches = sorted(df['batch'].unique())

print("=" * 70)
print("DETAILED PER-BATCH REGRESSION DIAGNOSTICS")
print("=" * 70)

for batch in batches:
    sub = df[df['batch'] == batch].copy().reset_index(drop=True)
    X = sm.add_constant(sub['dosage_mg'])
    model = sm.OLS(sub['response'], X).fit()
    
    print(f"\n{'='*50}")
    print(f"  {batch}")
    print(f"{'='*50}")
    print(model.summary())
    
    resid = model.resid
    shapiro_stat, shapiro_p = stats.shapiro(resid)
    print(f"\nShapiro-Wilk normality test: stat={shapiro_stat:.4f}, p={shapiro_p:.4f}")
    
    bp_stat, bp_p, _, _ = het_breuschpagan(resid, X)
    print(f"Breusch-Pagan test: stat={bp_stat:.4f}, p={bp_p:.4f}")
    
    dw = durbin_watson(resid)
    print(f"Durbin-Watson: {dw:.4f}")
    
    influence = model.get_influence()
    cooks_d = influence.cooks_distance[0]
    leverage = influence.hat_matrix_diag
    print(f"Max Cook's distance: {cooks_d.max():.4f} (observation idx: {cooks_d.idxmax()})")
    print(f"Max leverage: {leverage.max():.4f}")
    
    # Quadratic model
    dosage_sq = sub['dosage_mg'] ** 2
    X_quad = sm.add_constant(np.column_stack([sub['dosage_mg'].values, dosage_sq.values]))
    model_quad = sm.OLS(sub['response'].values, X_quad).fit()
    print(f"\nQuadratic term p-value: {model_quad.pvalues[2]:.4f}")
    print(f"Quadratic model R²: {model_quad.rsquared:.4f}")
    print(f"Linear model R²: {model.rsquared:.4f}")
    print(f"AIC linear: {model.aic:.2f}, AIC quadratic: {model_quad.aic:.2f}")

print("\n" + "=" * 70)
print("ANOVA: RESPONSE BY BATCH (controlling for dosage)")
print("=" * 70)
X_full = pd.get_dummies(df[['dosage_mg', 'batch']], columns=['batch'], drop_first=True, dtype=float)
X_full = sm.add_constant(X_full)
model_full = sm.OLS(df['response'], X_full).fit()
print(model_full.summary())

print("\n" + "=" * 70)
print("COVARIATE EFFECTS: lab_temp_c, weight_kg, technician")
print("=" * 70)
for cov in ['lab_temp_c', 'weight_kg']:
    r, p = stats.pearsonr(df[cov], df['response'])
    print(f"  {cov} vs response: r={r:.3f}, p={p:.3f}")

groups = [df[df['technician'] == t]['response'] for t in df['technician'].unique()]
f_stat, f_p = stats.f_oneway(*groups)
print(f"  Technician ANOVA: F={f_stat:.3f}, p={f_p:.3f}")

h_stat, h_p = stats.kruskal(*groups)
print(f"  Technician Kruskal-Wallis: H={h_stat:.3f}, p={h_p:.3f}")

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from scipy import stats
import statsmodels.api as sm
from statsmodels.stats.diagnostic import het_breuschpagan
from statsmodels.stats.stattools import durbin_watson

df = pd.read_csv('dataset.csv')
batches = sorted(df['batch'].unique())

print("=" * 70)
print("DETAILED PER-BATCH REGRESSION DIAGNOSTICS")
print("=" * 70)

for batch in batches:
    sub = df[df['batch'] == batch].copy().reset_index(drop=True)
    X = sm.add_constant(sub['dosage_mg'])
    model = sm.OLS(sub['response'], X).fit()
    
    print(f"\n{'='*50}")
    print(f"  {batch}")
    print(f"{'='*50}")
    print(model.summary())
    
    resid = model.resid
    shapiro_stat, shapiro_p = stats.shapiro(resid)
    print(f"\nShapiro-Wilk normality test: stat={shapiro_stat:.4f}, p={shapiro_p:.4f}")
    
    bp_stat, bp_p, _, _ = het_breuschpagan(resid, X)
    print(f"Breusch-Pagan test: stat={bp_stat:.4f}, p={bp_p:.4f}")
    
    dw = durbin_watson(resid)
    print(f"Durbin-Watson: {dw:.4f}")
    
    influence = model.get_influence()
    cooks_d = influence.cooks_distance[0]
    leverage = influence.hat_matrix_diag
    print(f"Max Cook's distance: {cooks_d.max():.4f} (observation idx: {np.argmax(cooks_d)})")
    print(f"Max leverage: {leverage.max():.4f}")
    
    # Quadratic model
    dosage_sq = sub['dosage_mg'].values ** 2
    X_quad = sm.add_constant(np.column_stack([sub['dosage_mg'].values, dosage_sq]))
    model_quad = sm.OLS(sub['response'].values, X_quad).fit()
    print(f"\nQuadratic term p-value: {model_quad.pvalues[2]:.4f}")
    print(f"Quadratic model R²: {model_quad.rsquared:.4f}")
    print(f"Linear model R²: {model.rsquared:.4f}")
    print(f"AIC linear: {model.aic:.2f}, AIC quadratic: {model_quad.aic:.2f}")

print("\n" + "=" * 70)
print("ANOVA: RESPONSE BY BATCH (controlling for dosage)")
print("=" * 70)
X_full = pd.get_dummies(df[['dosage_mg', 'batch']], columns=['batch'], drop_first=True, dtype=float)
X_full = sm.add_constant(X_full)
model_full = sm.OLS(df['response'], X_full).fit()
print(model_full.summary())

print("\n" + "=" * 70)
print("COVARIATE EFFECTS")
print("=" * 70)
for cov in ['lab_temp_c', 'weight_kg']:
    r, p = stats.pearsonr(df[cov], df['response'])
    print(f"  {cov} vs response: r={r:.3f}, p={p:.3f}")

groups = [df[df['technician'] == t]['response'] for t in df['technician'].unique()]
f_stat, f_p = stats.f_oneway(*groups)
print(f"  Technician ANOVA: F={f_stat:.3f}, p={f_p:.3f}")
